# Label Guide

Label taxonomy for the docs-v2 repository. Used by automation workflows,
triage agents, and human contributors.

## Product Labels (11) — `#FFA500`

Map content paths to products. Applied automatically by the
[auto-label workflow](workflows/auto-label.yml) using
[data/products.yml](../data/products.yml) as the source of truth.

| Label | Description | Content Paths |
|-------|-------------|--------------|
| `product:v3-monolith` | InfluxDB 3 Core and Enterprise | `influxdb3/core/`, `influxdb3/enterprise/` |
| `product:v3-distributed` | InfluxDB 3 Cloud Serverless, Cloud Dedicated, Clustered | `influxdb3/cloud-serverless/`, `influxdb3/cloud-dedicated/`, `influxdb3/clustered/` |
| `product:v2` | InfluxDB v2 (OSS, Cloud TSM) | `influxdb/v2/`, `influxdb/cloud/` |
| `product:v1` | InfluxDB OSS v1 | `influxdb/v1/` |
| `product:v1-enterprise` | InfluxDB Enterprise v1 | `enterprise_influxdb/` |
| `product:telegraf` | Telegraf | `telegraf/` |
| `product:chronograf` | Chronograf | `chronograf/` |
| `product:kapacitor` | Kapacitor | `kapacitor/` |
| `product:flux` | Flux language | `flux/` |
| `product:explorer` | InfluxDB 3 Explorer | `influxdb3/explorer/` |
| `product:shared` | Shared content across products | `shared/` |

**Multi-product PRs:** All matching labels are applied additively.

**Shared content:** `product:shared` is applied plus all product labels for
pages that reference the shared file.

## Source Labels (4) — `#9370DB`

Track how an issue or PR was created.

| Label | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `source:auto-detected` | Created by change detection within this repo |
| `source:dar` | Generated by the DAR pipeline |
| `source:sync` | Synced from an external repository (replaces `sync-plugin-docs`) |
| `source:manual` | Human-created issue |

## Waiting Labels (2) — `#FF8C00`

Indicate external blockers.

| Label | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `waiting:engineering` | Waiting for engineer confirmation |
| `waiting:product` | Waiting for product or PM decision |

## Workflow Labels (2) — `#00FF00` / `#1E90FF`

Control automation behavior.

| Label | Color | Description |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| `agent-ready` | `#00FF00` | Issue can be worked autonomously by an agent |
| `skip-review` | `#1E90FF` | PR skips the automated doc review pipeline |

## Review Labels (3)

Automated review outcomes. Mutually exclusive — the doc-review workflow
removes old `review:*` labels before applying a new one.

| Label | Color | Description |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| `review:approved` | `#28A745` | Automated review passed |
| `review:changes-requested` | `#DC3545` | Automated review found blocking issues |
| `review:needs-human` | `#FFC107` | Automated review inconclusive |

Human approval uses GitHub's native PR review system (CODEOWNERS), not labels.

## Renamed Labels (2)

| Old Name | New Name | Color |
|----------|----------|-------|
| `AI assistant tooling` | `ai:tooling` | (unchanged) |
| `ci:testing-and-validation` | `ci:testing` | (unchanged) |

## Common Workflows

### Issue triage

1. Read issue → identify product(s) → apply `product:*` labels
2. Apply `source:*` label if applicable
3. Determine readiness → apply `agent-ready` or `waiting:*`

### PR review pipeline

1. PR opened → auto-label applies `product:*` labels
2. Doc review workflow triggers (unless `skip-review` is present)
3. Claude reviews diff → applies `review:approved`, `review:changes-requested`,
   or `review:needs-human`
4. Copilot reviews rendered preview pages
5. Human reviewer uses GitHub's PR review for final approval

### GitHub Filter Queries

```
# PRs needing human review
label:review:needs-human is:pr is:open

# Agent-ready issues
label:agent-ready is:issue is:open -label:waiting:engineering -label:waiting:product

# All InfluxDB 3 issues
label:product:v3-monolith,product:v3-distributed is:issue is:open

# Blocked issues
label:waiting:engineering,waiting:product is:issue is:open

# PRs that skipped review
label:skip-review is:pr
```

## Auto-labeling Behavior

The [auto-label workflow](workflows/auto-label.yml) runs on
`pull_request: [opened, synchronize]` and:

- Reads path-to-product mappings from `data/products.yml`
- Matches changed files to product labels
- Expands shared content changes to affected product labels
- Adds labels idempotently (skips labels already present)
- Skips draft and fork PRs
